circuits_skipped_reason.xor: shallow circuit; SV1+dm1 already cover; budget conservation
circuits_skipped_reason.copy: Φ_proxy identically 0 (redundant fanout); no substrate-discrimination value

and.decomposition: ccnot decomposed into 6 cnot + 9 single-qubit (h, t, ti) — IonQ Forte 1 native gate set has no Toffoli
and.noise_signature: 2% leakage into 101 + 011; 4 expected outcomes at 22-27% (vs noiseless 25%) — within Forte 1 fidelity envelope (6 CNOT × 0.9922 = 0.954, observed 0.98 in dominant outcomes)

maj.noise_signature: 18 CNOT × 0.9922 = 0.868 expected; observed top-5 mass = 0.75; some redistribution but Φ_proxy converges to within 1.3% of SV1 noiseless value — substrate-invariance signal strong even at this gate depth

honest_c3: 100 shots = ±5% binomial sampling noise floor; Φ_proxy precision ±0.05 bits
AND comparison vs SV1 used 3-qubit projection of SV1 result for apples-to-apples
Toffoli decomposition adds ~6× (AND) and 18× (MAJ) gate depth vs simulator's ccnot — noise penalty is decomposition-dependent
Forte 1 has no Toffoli, so cross-substrate comparison embeds the choice of decomposition
